proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter pfs_months on average than those with stage_iv = 0.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.linregress(df['ecog_ps'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Linear regression of pfs_months on ecog_ps yields slope=-1.190 months per ECOG unit, p<1e-300 (extremely significant). Higher ECOG strongly predicts shorter PFS, as expected clinically.
Mean pfs_months in stage_iv=1 (n=27,481) is 1.352 months lower than stage_iv=0 (n=22,519), Welch t-test p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.465 months per g/dL of albumin, p=3.2e-115. Higher albumin strongly predicts longer PFS.
Slope = -0.073 months per 1% weight loss, p=2.2e-167. Greater weight loss strongly predicts shorter PFS.
Slope = -0.0053 months per ng/mL CEA, p=3.1e-17. Higher CEA modestly but significantly predicts shorter PFS.
Slope = -0.0003 months per U/L LDH, p=0.013. Higher LDH significantly predicts shorter PFS, with small per-unit effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation = 0.
braf_v600e = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e = 0.
nras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation = 0.
right_sided_primary = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary = 0 (left-sided).

analyses.result_summary: KRAS-mutant patients (n=20,898) have mean PFS 0.327 months lower than KRAS-WT (n=29,102), Welch t-test p=1.9e-58.
BRAF V600E-mutant patients (n=2,272) have mean PFS 0.228 months lower than BRAF-WT (n=47,728), p=3.5e-7.

Surprise: NRAS-mutant patients (n=1,506) have mean PFS 0.221 months HIGHER than NRAS-WT (n=48,494), p=7.3e-4. Direction opposite to hypothesis. After adjusting for KRAS/BRAF/right-sidedness/ECOG/stage/age/albumin/weight loss, the effect attenuates to +0.030 (p=0.23, NS), suggesting confounding.
Right-sided primary tumors (n=17,657) have mean PFS 0.305 months lower than left-sided (n=32,343), p=1.0e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: msi_high = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high = 0 (better prognosis hypothesis).
her2_amplified = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified = 0.
ntrk_fusion = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than ntrk_fusion = 0.

analyses.result_summary: MSI-high vs MSI-stable PFS difference is -0.019 months (n_msi=2,513), Welch t-test p=0.68. No detectable main effect of MSI status on PFS in this cohort.
HER2-amplified vs not: PFS difference -0.056 months (n_her2=1,504), p=0.36. No main effect of HER2 amplification on PFS.
NTRK fusion vs not: PFS difference +0.040 months (n_ntrk=251), p=0.79. No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope), reflecting frailty.
sex_female = 1 is associated with different pfs_months than sex_female = 0.

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df['age_years'], df['pfs_months']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + age_sq', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis. Slope = +0.176 months per year, p<1e-300; effect persists after adjustment (+0.176 multivariable). Quadratic age term is also strongly positive (p=7e-34), so PFS rises convexly with age. Older patients in this cohort have LONGER PFS, not shorter — possibly reflecting selection bias toward fitter older patients or a synthetic-data artifact. Hypothesis refuted.
Mean PFS difference female-male = -0.006 months, p=0.77. No sex difference in PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model with age, sex, ECOG, stage, sidedness, key biomarkers, CEA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, and all six treatments, the disease-severity terms (ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin, weight loss) remain the dominant predictors of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.859. Strongest negative predictors: stage_iv beta=-1.36 (p<<0.001), ecog_ps beta=-1.17 (p<<0.001), weight_loss_pct_6mo beta=-0.076 (p<<0.001), kras_mutation beta=-0.354, braf_v600e beta=-0.357, right_sided_primary beta=-0.287, cea_ng_ml beta=-0.0050. Strongest positive: age_years beta=+0.176, albumin_g_dl beta=+0.469, treatment_regorafenib beta=+0.943 (p<<0.001). Other treatments NS. Hypothesis supported: severity terms dominate; only one treatment (regorafenib) has a non-trivial main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab on (n=15,078) vs off (n=34,922): unadjusted PFS difference -0.038, p=0.10. Adjusted (multivariable model from h16): beta=-0.011, p=0.19. No PFS benefit; if anything a tiny non-significant decrement.
Bevacizumab on (n=22,383) vs off (n=27,617): unadjusted diff -0.019, p=0.35. Adjusted beta=+0.003, p=0.73. No PFS benefit.

Regorafenib on (n=10,022) vs off (n=39,978): unadjusted PFS difference +0.998 months. In the full multivariable model, beta=+0.943, t=97.9, p<<0.001 — by far the largest treatment effect in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_cetuximab benefit (PFS gain) is restricted to KRAS-wild-type patients (kras_mutation=0), reflecting standard CRC anti-EGFR biology — i.e., a positive treatment_cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction with cetuximab helping only when kras_mutation=0.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab*kras_mutation + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab main effect beta=-0.021 (p=0.085); kras_mutation main effect beta=-0.333 (p=3e-223); interaction beta=+0.021 (p=0.27). Stratified: KRAS-WT cetux diff=-0.048 (p=0.12), KRAS-mut cetux diff=-0.028 (p=0.38). No significant cetuximab benefit in KRAS-WT and no interaction. Hypothesis refuted — cetuximab does not behave as expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the cetuximab-eligible subgroup (KRAS-WT, NRAS-WT, BRAF-WT), treatment_cetuximab confers longer pfs_months in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than right-sided patients — i.e., positive cetuximab x left-sided interaction.

analyses.code: sub = df[(df['kras_mutation']==0)&(df['nras_mutation']==0)&(df['braf_v600e']==0)]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab*right_sided_primary + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the triple-WT subgroup (n=27,148): cetuximab beta=-0.039 (p=0.067), right-sided beta=-0.549 (p=5e-165), interaction beta=+0.040 (p=0.27). Stratified within triple-WT: left-sided cetux diff=-0.065 (p=0.11), right-sided cetux diff=-0.031 (p=0.49). No interaction; cetuximab provides no benefit even in the canonically-eligible subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab confers longer pfs_months specifically in MSI-high (msi_high=1) patients — positive pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*msi_high + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab main beta=-0.003 (p=0.84), MSI-high main beta=-0.021 (p=0.33), interaction beta=+0.060 (p=0.28). Stratified: MSI-high pembro diff=+0.007 (p=0.96, n_pembro=396), MSI-stable pembro diff=+0.005 (p=0.86). No interaction and no benefit in MSI-high. Hypothesis refuted — the dataset shows no immunotherapy efficacy signal in MSI-high.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_encorafenib confers longer pfs_months specifically in BRAF V600E mutant patients — positive encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction.
Treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib confers longer pfs_months specifically in HER2-amplified patients — positive trastuzumab/tucatinib x her2_amplified interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib main beta=-0.004 (p=0.79), BRAF V600E main beta=-0.195 (p=1e-18), interaction beta=+7e-5 (p=0.999). Stratified: BRAF-mut encorafenib diff=-0.129 (p=0.33, n=242), BRAF-WT encorafenib diff=+0.013 (p=0.72). No benefit in BRAF V600E. Hypothesis refuted.

Trastuzumab/tucatinib main beta=+0.020 (p=0.23), HER2-amplified main beta=-0.008 (p=0.77), interaction beta=-0.007 (p=0.94). Stratified: HER2-amp trastuzumab/tucatinib diff=+0.017 (p=0.93), HER2-neg diff=-0.046 (p=0.23). No benefit in HER2-amplified subgroup. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Given that the canonical biomarker-targeted treatments do not show their expected interactions, treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit may itself be heterogeneous across the KRAS-mutation subgroup. Specifically, regorafenib benefit is larger in kras_mutation = 0 than in kras_mutation = 1 — i.e., a positive regorafenib x KRAS-WT interaction (negative regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib*kras_mutation + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib main beta=+1.628 (p<<0.001 in KRAS-WT), kras_mutation beta=-0.028, interaction (treatment_regorafenib:kras_mutation) beta=-1.635 (p<<0.001). Stratified: KRAS-WT regorafenib diff=+1.656 (p=6e-293, n=29,102), KRAS-mut regorafenib diff=+0.027 (p=0.46). The +0.94-month main effect is entirely driven by KRAS-WT patients; in KRAS-mut patients regorafenib has no measurable PFS effect. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit is also restricted by BRAF status: benefit is larger in braf_v600e = 0 than in braf_v600e = 1, mirroring the KRAS pattern.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib*braf_v600e + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib main beta=+0.989 (p<<0.001), BRAF V600E beta=-0.155 (p=2e-13), interaction beta=-0.987 (p=2e-102). Stratified: BRAF-WT regorafenib diff=+1.027 (p=5e-226), BRAF-mut regorafenib diff=-0.156 (p=0.13, n=468). Regorafenib benefit is essentially absent in BRAF V600E-mutant patients. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit is restricted to left-sided primaries: benefit is larger when right_sided_primary = 0 than when right_sided_primary = 1 (negative regorafenib x right-sided interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib*right_sided_primary + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + braf_v600e', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib main beta=+1.454 (in left-sided), right-sided beta=+0.005 (p=0.60), interaction beta=-1.461 (p<<0.001). Stratified: left-sided regorafenib diff=+1.480 (p=4e-274), right-sided regorafenib diff=+0.026 (p=0.52). The benefit is essentially confined to left-sided primaries.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining the three findings, treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in the joint subgroup KRAS-WT AND left-sided (and BRAF-WT) — i.e., the three-way regorafenib x kras_mutation x right_sided_primary interaction is significant, and benefit is essentially zero in any cell that is not (kras=0, right=0).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib*kras_mutation*right_sided_primary + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + braf_v600e', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction beta=+2.510 (p<<0.001). Cell-wise stratified estimates: KRAS=0 & left-sided regorafenib diff=+2.520 (p<<0.001, n=18,853); KRAS=0 & right-sided +0.071 (p=0.16); KRAS=1 & left-sided +0.061 (p=0.17); KRAS=1 & right-sided -0.040 (p=0.52). Benefit is strikingly localized to the KRAS-WT/left-sided cell. In the further-restricted triple-WT (KRAS-/NRAS-/BRAF-WT) AND left-sided subgroup (n=16,443), regorafenib diff=+2.749 months (p<<0.001). Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: PFS differs by race_ethnicity even after adjusting for clinical/lab confounders (i.e., disparity hypothesis).
PFS differs by insurance_type (e.g., uninsured/medicaid have shorter PFS than private), even after adjustment.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['race_ethnicity']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in df['race_ethnicity'].unique()]); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(race_ethnicity) + ...', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA across race categories: F-test p=0.48. Adjusted multivariable: each race coefficient |beta|<0.02, all p>0.5. No detectable racial disparity in PFS. Hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway across insurance levels; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(insurance_type) + ...', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA p=0.22. Adjusted: medicare beta=-0.007 (p=0.54), private beta=-0.003 (p=0.78), uninsured beta=-0.027 (p=0.21), all relative to medicaid. No detectable insurance disparity in PFS. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of biomarker-targeted treatments (treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib, treatment_regorafenib) differs by insurance_type — uninsured/medicaid patients receive these less often than private/medicare.
Receipt of these treatments differs by race_ethnicity (disparity in access/use).

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square tests for each treatment x insurance: cetuximab p=0.55, bevacizumab p=0.41, pembrolizumab p=0.67, encorafenib p=0.90, trastuzumab/tucatinib p=0.47, regorafenib p=0.59. Treatment receipt rates within ±1 percentage point across insurance groups. No insurance-based access disparities. Hypothesis refuted.

Chi-square treatment x race tests: pembrolizumab p=0.45, regorafenib p=0.55, encorafenib p=0.078 (NS at 0.05 with five comparisons), trastuzumab/tucatinib p=0.95. No race-based differential treatment receipt. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in rural_residence = 1 have shorter pfs_months than urban (rural-urban disparity).
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Rural vs urban PFS difference = +0.024 months, p=0.34. No rural disparity. Hypothesis refuted.
Slope = +0.0033 months per year of education, p=0.34. No detectable association. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 27 SNPs (snp_*) is significantly associated with pfs_months after Bonferroni correction (alpha = 0.05/27 ≈ 1.85e-3).

analyses.result_summary: Top SNPs by p-value: snp_rs1050828 (diff=+0.092, p=0.017), snp_rs1801131 (diff=-0.049, p=0.027), snp_rs1801197 (diff=-0.049, p=0.046). Bonferroni cutoff = 1.85e-3. No SNP meets the cutoff. Hypothesis refuted — no convincing pharmacogenomic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher symptom-burden grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months independently of ECOG/stage/albumin/weight loss.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ fatigue_grade + pain_nrs + dyspnea_grade + cough_grade + appetite_loss_grade + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All five symptom betas |beta|<0.005 with p-values 0.24-0.85. Once ECOG, stage, albumin, and weight loss are in the model, individual symptom grades carry no additional independent prognostic information. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, depression_anxiety_diagnosis) shorten pfs_months independent of age/ECOG/stage/albumin/weight loss.

analyses.code: for comorb in [...]: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {comorb} + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Comorbidity adjusted betas (months): diabetes +0.010 (p=0.33), hypertension +0.006 (p=0.53), copd -0.027 (p=0.0045), CKD +0.007 (p=0.62), heart failure +0.009 (p=0.54), CAD -0.005 (p=0.64), atrial fibrillation -0.017 (p=0.28), VTE +0.025 (p=0.18), depression/anxiety -0.008 (p=0.47). Only COPD reaches nominal p<0.05 (modest -0.027 months). Hypothesis essentially refuted; comorbidities have minimal independent effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_bevacizumab interacts with braf_v600e: in BRAF V600E patients, bevacizumab is associated with shorter PFS than in BRAF-WT.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified raw: BRAF-mut bevacizumab diff=-0.237 (p=0.0063, n=2,272); BRAF-WT bevacizumab diff=-0.010 (p=0.65). Multivariable interaction term: bevacizumab beta=+0.005, BRAF beta=-0.337, interaction beta=-0.032 (p=0.43). The raw stratified signal does not survive adjustment — likely confounding by other covariates (KRAS, sidedness, severity). Hypothesis weakly supported in raw analysis but not in adjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS penalty associated with each unit increase in ecog_ps is smaller (less negative) at older ages — i.e., positive age x ECOG interaction (older patients are 'pre-selected' for fitness so ECOG matters less).
stage_iv x ecog_ps interaction is non-zero (the disease-severity contributions of stage and ECOG are not strictly additive).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years*ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Age main beta=+0.182, ECOG main beta=-0.734, age:ecog_ps interaction beta=-0.0069 (p=1.2e-26). Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis: each year of age makes the ECOG penalty MORE negative (older + worse ECOG is worse than additive). Hypothesis refuted but interaction is real.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps*stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: ecog_ps main beta=-1.203, stage_iv main beta=-1.396, ecog_ps:stage_iv interaction beta=+0.049 (p=1.0e-4). Significant positive interaction: the per-ECOG-unit decrement in PFS is slightly less severe in stage-IV patients (small magnitude, large sample). Hypothesis supported, modest effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all clinical/lab confounders AND the other five treatments, treatment_regorafenib's effect within the KRAS-WT, left-sided subgroup remains a large positive PFS gain (>=2 months).

analyses.code: sub = df[(df['kras_mutation']==0)&(df['right_sided_primary']==0)]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + cea_ng_ml + ldh_u_l + braf_v600e + nras_mutation + msi_high + her2_amplified + treatment_cetuximab + treatment_bevacizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_encorafenib + treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib', data=sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the KRAS-WT, left-sided subgroup (n=18,853), the fully-adjusted regorafenib beta=+2.516 months (p<<0.001), with model R^2=0.818. The effect is robust to all clinical/lab confounders and to inclusion of the five other treatments. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: in this dataset, of the six treatments, only treatment_regorafenib has a clinically meaningful PFS effect, and that effect is concentrated in the EGFR-favorable subgroup (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided). The five canonical biomarker-targeted indications (cetuximab in KRAS-WT/left-sided, pembrolizumab in MSI-H, encorafenib in BRAF V600E, trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amp, bevacizumab broadly) are not reproduced.

Treatments are assigned approximately at random with respect to biomarkers (no chi-square evidence of biomarker-guided assignment), suggesting this cohort behaves like a simulated/randomized cohort rather than real-world biomarker-driven prescribing.

analyses.result_summary: Across all six treatments, only regorafenib has a non-trivial main effect on PFS (+0.94 months adjusted, p<<0.001). Cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib all have |beta|<0.03 months and p>0.05 in the multivariable model AND in their indicated biomarker subgroups. Regorafenib's effect is concentrated in KRAS-WT/left-sided/BRAF-WT (+2.5 to +2.7 months), which paradoxically coincides with the canonical anti-EGFR-eligible subgroup rather than salvage TKI biology. Hypothesis supported.

Across 30 treatment x biomarker chi-square tests (6 treatments x 5 key biomarkers), only treatment_bevacizumab x braf_v600e is nominally significant (p=0.040, 42.6% vs 44.9%) — a magnitude of imbalance consistent with random assignment plus 30 multiple comparisons. Treatment receipt rates differ by <2 percentage points across biomarker, race, and insurance strata. Hypothesis supported: treatments behave as if randomly assigned with respect to biomarkers and demographics in this cohort.
